section.heading: Use of funds
section.chapeau: An eligible entity may use a grant received under this part to carry out activities to achieve the purposes of the program, including—

paragraph.content: development and implementation of an energy efficiency and conservation strategy under section 17155(b) of this title;

paragraph.chapeau: retaining technical consultant services to assist the eligible entity in the development of such a strategy, including—

subparagraph.content: formulation of energy efficiency, energy conservation, and energy usage goals;

subparagraph.chapeau: identification of strategies to achieve those goals—

clause.content: through efforts to increase energy efficiency and reduce energy consumption; and
by encouraging behavioral changes among the population served by the eligible entity;

subparagraph.content: development of methods to measure progress in achieving the goals;

subparagraph.chapeau: development and publication of annual reports to the population served by the eligible entity describing—

clause.content: the progress made in achieving the strategies and goals during the preceding calendar year; and

subparagraph.content: other services to assist in the implementation of the energy efficiency and conservation strategy;

paragraph.content: conducting residential and commercial building energy audits;
establishment of financial incentive programs for energy efficiency improvements;
the provision of grants to nonprofit organizations and governmental agencies for the purpose of performing energy efficiency retrofits;

paragraph.chapeau: development and implementation of energy efficiency and conservation programs for buildings and facilities within the jurisdiction of the eligible entity, including—

subparagraph.content: design and operation of the programs;
identifying the most effective methods for achieving maximum participation and efficiency rates;

paragraph.chapeau: development and implementation of programs to conserve energy used in transportation, including—

subparagraph.content: use of flex time by employers;
development and promotion of zoning guidelines or requirements that promote energy efficient development;
development of infrastructure, such as bike lanes and pathways and pedestrian walkways;
other measures that increase energy efficiency and decrease energy consumption;

paragraph.content: development and implementation of building codes and inspection services to promote building energy efficiency;

paragraph.chapeau: application and implementation of energy distribution technologies that significantly increase energy efficiency, including—

paragraph.content: activities to increase participation and efficiency rates for material conservation programs, including source reduction, recycling, and recycled content procurement programs that lead to increases in energy efficiency;
the purchase and implementation of technologies to reduce, capture, and, to the maximum extent practicable, use methane and other greenhouse gases generated by landfills or similar sources;

paragraph.chapeau: replacement of traffic signals and street lighting with energy efficient lighting technologies, including—

subparagraph.content: any other technology of equal or greater energy efficiency;

paragraph.chapeau: development, implementation, and installation on or in any government building of the eligible entity of onsite renewable energy technology that generates electricity from renewable resources, including—

paragraph.content: programs for financing energy efficiency, renewable energy, and zero-emission transportation (and associated infrastructure), capital investments, projects, and programs, which may include loan programs and performance contracting programs, for leveraging of additional public and private sector funds, and programs that allow rebates, grants, or other incentives for the purchase and installation of energy efficiency, renewable energy, and zero-emission transportation (and associated infrastructure) measures; and

paragraph.chapeau: any other appropriate activity, as determined by the Secretary, in consultation with—

subparagraph.content: the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency;
the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2021—Pars. (14), (15). Pub. L. 117–58 added par. (14) and redesignated former par. (14) as (15).

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective on the date that is 1 day after Dec. 19, 2007, see section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140, set out as a note under section 1824 of Title 2, The Congress.

note.heading: Wage Rate Requirements
note.p: For provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of Pub. L. 117–58, including authority of Secretary of Labor, see section 18851 of this title.
